name: Axure RP Prototyping Tool API
what_it_does: Professional UX prototyping and wireframing tool with limited cloud API — enabling UX designers to create high-fidelity interactive prototypes with conditional logic, dynamic content, adaptive views (responsive design), and complex interactions without code, with Axure Cloud for sharing and feedback collection.
use_cases: Publishing Axure prototypes to Axure Cloud for stakeholder review and feedback
Managing Axure Cloud workspaces and team members via API for enterprise license management
Sharing prototype URLs and collecting stakeholder feedback via Axure Cloud review links
Integrating Axure with project management tools via Axure Cloud API
Querying Axure Cloud prototype comment and feedback data for UX research documentation
not_for: Rapid low-fidelity wireframing (use Balsamiq for quick concept sketching)
Design system management and developer handoff (use Figma for design systems and specs)
Real-time collaboration on the same file (Axure cloud sync has lag; use Figma for live collab)
best_when: A senior UX designer or enterprise UX team needs to create complex, high-fidelity prototypes with conditional logic and realistic interactions — especially for enterprise software products, government applications, or complex transactional systems.
avoid_when: You need real-time collaboration, simple mockups, design-to-code handoff, or are early stage where iteration speed matters more than prototype fidelity.

auth.notes: API key from Axure Cloud account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No webhooks — polling required. Axure RP Desktop application is the design tool — Axure Cloud API manages sharing and collaboration. No real-time collaboration API (Axure RP is file-based). No OAuth2.

pricing.notes: San Diego company. Founded 2002. Bootstrapped and profitable. UX prototyping market pioneer. 200,000+ users in enterprise. Strong in government, healthcare, financial services. Desktop application (Axure RP) is primary tool — cloud is for sharing. Figma has significantly disrupted Axure's market since 2018. Axure advantage: complex conditional logic and adaptive views that Figma's prototype mode doesn't match.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited Axure Cloud REST API for workspace and prototype management. API key bearer auth. Limited public API documentation. Axure RP (desktop) has no API — only Axure Cloud collaboration features. Prototype content creation requires desktop application. No webhooks. Primary use case is prototype sharing and team management, not content creation via API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workspace reads fully idempotent. Prototype reads idempotent. Comment reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Axure RP (design tool) is desktop application — no API for creating/editing prototypes
API only covers Axure Cloud collaboration features (sharing, comments, teams)
No webhooks — must poll for comment and review status updates
File-based design (not real-time) means concurrent editing conflicts without proper workflow
Figma market disruption — evaluate if Axure's complex conditional logic is truly needed

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. API key auth. Prototype content may contain confidential product designs and UX flows — restrict sharing links and API access. On-premises option (Axure Share) available for highest IP protection requirements. Cloud prototypes are publicly accessible via shared link by default — configure access controls.
